# VERIFY: #70 — feat: design Vercel-compatible reference architecture for LTM knowledge layer
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Verify that the implemented adapter enables Meridian agents and skills to read LTM content correctly in a Vercel serverless environment while confirming no regression in local behaviour.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All LTM file reads that currently succeed locally (`analyze-changes`, `analyze-pr`, `manage-issue`, `repo-orchestrator`, `product-strategist`) continue to succeed locally with no change to invocation
- [ ] The same skills and agents, when executed in a Vercel function context, successfully retrieve LTM content via Vercel Blob (not the filesystem)
- [ ] `sync-claude --vercel` uploads all files under `core/components/memory/` to Vercel Blob under the corresponding key paths
- [ ] A missing LTM key in Blob produces a loud, structured failure (not a silent fallback or undefined behaviour)
- [ ] ADR 011 is authored, covers context/decision/consequences, and references ADRs 007 and 009 correctly
- [ ] `core/config.yaml` contains the Vercel LTM configuration block
- [ ] No skill definition file (`SKILL.md`) has been modified — the path strings in skills remain unchanged

## Verification Steps

| Step | Method | Expected Outcome | Gate | Mandatory |
|------|--------|-----------------|------|-----------|
| V1: Local regression — analyze-changes | Invoke `analyze-changes` skill locally; confirm `~/.meridian/core/memory/standards/commits/categories.md` is read | Skill produces analysis output without error | G1 | Yes |
| V2: Local regression — manage-issue | Invoke `manage-issue` with `action: create`; confirm template loads from `formats/github-issue.md` | Issue body uses template structure | G1 | Yes |
| V3: Blob upload verification | Run `sync-claude --vercel`; inspect Vercel Blob store | All LTM files appear as Blob objects with correct key paths | G2 | Yes |
| V4: Vercel runtime LTM read | Deploy Vercel function invoking `fetch-ltm` with `logical_path: standards/commits/categories.md` | Function returns file content with HTTP 200; matches source file | G2 | Yes |
| V5: Missing key behaviour | Invoke `fetch-ltm` with non-existent path in Vercel context | Structured failure with `file_not_found` signal; not silent | G3 | Yes |
| V6: ADR 011 completeness | Read `docs/adr/011-ltm-vercel-adapter.md` | Contains: Status, Date, Context (root cause), Decision, Consequences, Related ADRs (007, 009) | G4 | Yes |
| V7: No skill modifications | `git diff main -- core/components/skills/` | Zero changes to any `SKILL.md` (except `sync-claude` and new `fetch-ltm`) | G1 | Yes |
| V8: `core/config.yaml` updated | Read `core/config.yaml` | Contains `vercel-ltm` block with `blob-store`, `key-prefix`, `runtime-env-var` | G4 | No |
| V9: Agent conditional branch | Read `repo-orchestrator.md` Memory section | Contains runtime dispatch: check `VERCEL` env var; if set, invoke `fetch-ltm`; else `Read` directly | G2 | Yes |
| V10: Latency acceptance | Measure `fetch-ltm` response time in deployed warm Vercel function | P95 latency < 200ms for single LTM file fetch | G3 | No |

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behavior |
|----------|------------------|
| Vercel cold start — Blob not yet populated | `fetch-ltm` returns structured failure `ltm_not_deployed`; agent surfaces clear error asking adopter to run `sync-claude --vercel` |
| New LTM file added locally, `sync-claude --vercel` not re-run | `fetch-ltm` returns `file_not_found` for the new key; loud failure per ADR 009 rule 4 |
| LTM file updated locally and `sync-claude --vercel` re-run | Blob object overwritten; next invocation reads new content |
| `VERCEL` env var set locally (e.g., in a test) | `fetch-ltm` checks for `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN`; if absent, falls back to local read with a warning |
| LTM file content exceeds Blob free tier | No size limit per Blob object; adoption cost negligible at current LTM scale (all files < 50KB) |
| `product-strategist` Glob + Grep search across `knowledge/` | Cannot Glob/Grep against Blob. Fetch `knowledge/_index.md` first; then fetch specific files by key. Bounded workaround, not a fundamental gap. |
